events.description: Officials from BIA Washington office, Billings Area Director's Office, and design architects and construction engineers from Branch of Plant Design and Construction in Albuquerque met to develop construction program for Busby School
End date of multi-day planning meeting for Busby School construction program
Northern Cheyenne Tribal Council adopted Land Development Program by Resolution 9(60)
Land enterprise loan of $50,000 approved for Northern Cheyenne Tribe
Funds for $50,000 land enterprise loan allotted to Northern Cheyenne Tribe
Delegation of Rocky Boy representatives visited Montana Delegation and BIA officials; informed about idle funds earmarked for Osage and Hopi
Executive Council meeting of Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians
End date of Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians Executive Council meeting
House Interior Committee reported H.R. 1150 to House Floor addressing Minnesota heirship land problem and $15M increase in Indian loan fund
Congress appropriated $40,000 for rehabilitation of Northern Cheyenne school facilities instead of requested $600,000
Expected start of active construction at Busby School at beginning of fiscal year 1962
Secretary Seaton stated no Indian tribe would be deprived of Federal supervision until it knew, understood, and accepted termination program
83rd Congress launched drive to terminate all Indian tribes without their consent

events.location: Rocky Boy's Agency, Box Elder, Montana
events.description: Chippewa Cree Tribe applied for revolving credit loan funds for reservation development program
Bureau of Indian Affairs potentially to re-advertise 8,894.05 acres of land on Northern Cheyenne Reservation
Northern Cheyenne Land Committee went on record recommending council purchase 894.05 acres

financial_transactions.description: Congressional appropriation for rehabilitation of Northern Cheyenne school facilities, considered inadequate by Education Committee
Original amount requested for classroom construction at Busby School, not granted
Existing authorization level of Indian Revolving Credit Loan Fund
Proposed new authorization level under S. 3546 introduced by Mansfield and Murray
Increase in Indian loan fund proposed under H.R. 1150 and recommended by Interior Department in report on S. 51
Amount requested by Edward Whiteman for appropriation bill to fund Indian land purchase programs
Land enterprise loan approved for Northern Cheyenne Tribe
Tribal funds supplementing the $50,000 land enterprise loan
Cash carried by Northern Cheyenne land enterprise at December 31, 1959
Loan amount contemplated by Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program
Amount by which the relevant part of the Indian revolving loan fund was overcommitted as of February 29, 1960
Funds earmarked for Osage and Hopi Indians lying idle in revolving credit fund, potentially available for Rocky Boy

correspondence.subject: School at Busby and increase in Indian Revolving Credit Loan Fund
correspondence.action_requested: Information sharing; enclosing BIA report on Busby school planning and Frank Church letter on revolving credit fund
correspondence.outcome: Informed Woodenlegs of progress on both matters

correspondence.sender_title: U.S. Senator, Idaho; Chairman, Indian Affairs Subcommittee
correspondence.subject: S. 3546 - increase in Indian revolving credit loan fund
correspondence.action_requested: Update on status of S. 3546 and H.R. 1150
correspondence.outcome: Reported awaiting Interior and Budget Bureau reports; noted H.R. 1150 before House Rules Committee

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Commissioner, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Status of planning for Northern Cheyenne School facilities at Busby, Montana
correspondence.action_requested: Returning enclosure as requested; providing update on Busby school planning
correspondence.outcome: Reported planning meeting held May 9-11; drawings to start after beginning of next fiscal year; planning complete by end of calendar year

correspondence.sender: Education Committee of the Busby School
correspondence.subject: Inadequate school facilities at Busby and insufficient appropriation
correspondence.action_requested: Requested senators get original $600,000 for classroom construction; expressed dissatisfaction with $40,000 appropriation
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Land sales on reservations and trust vs fee title
correspondence.action_requested: Informing Stevens of enclosed letter from Roger Ernst; indicating close follow-up
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded Ernst's April 20 letter to Stevens

correspondence.subject: Bryan Stevens inquiry about trust vs fee title for land purchases
correspondence.action_requested: Enclosing copy of reply to Stevens as requested
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded copy of reply to Stevens

correspondence.subject: Request to buy land in trust rather than fee status
correspondence.action_requested: Apologized for delay; indicated working on modification of policies on acquiring land in trust
correspondence.outcome: Promised worthwhile results pending policy review

correspondence.subject: Land sales on reservations
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledged Stevens letter; indicated following through on land sales matter
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded Stevens letter to Secretary of Interior

correspondence.subject: Bryan Stevens inquiry about trust vs fee title land purchases
correspondence.action_requested: Requested copy of response to Stevens; returned enclosure
correspondence.outcome: Roger Ernst responded April 20, 1960

correspondence.subject: Land development program for Northern Cheyenne Tribe
correspondence.action_requested: Requested BIA views on Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program; asked for return of enclosures
correspondence.outcome: BIA studied program; Billings Area Office instructed to assist tribe with loan application

correspondence.subject: Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program and tribal land purchase
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledged land program; asked if Tribal Council prepared to meet highest bid on 8,894.05 acres if re-advertised
correspondence.outcome: Woodenlegs responded February 29 confirming Land Committee recommended purchase

correspondence.subject: Proposed land program and 894.05 acres purchase
correspondence.action_requested: Confirmed Land Committee recommended purchase of 894.05 acres; noted funds available but process takes time; warned some owners may have applied for patent-in-fee
correspondence.outcome: Mansfield responded March 4, 1960

correspondence.subject: Northern Cheyenne land program
correspondence.action_requested: Assured continued support for land program proposal
correspondence.outcome: Continued advocacy

correspondence.subject: BIA instruction to Billings Area Office to assist with loan application
correspondence.action_requested: Forwarded letter from Assistant Secretary of Interior; encouraged continued work on program
correspondence.outcome: Informed Woodenlegs of BIA cooperation

correspondence.subject: Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program loan application
correspondence.action_requested: Reported Commissioner instructed Billings Area Office to assist tribe; noted fund overcommitted by $1,353,797
correspondence.outcome: Billings Area Office directed to work with tribe subject to fund availability

correspondence.subject: Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledged receipt of land program; returned program as requested; noted $50,000 loan approved July 1959; indicated BIA studying program
correspondence.outcome: Promised further correspondence; noted $550,000 loan not currently available

correspondence.subject: Northern Cheyenne Land Development Program
correspondence.action_requested: Forwarded Roger Ernst acknowledgment; assured continued support
correspondence.outcome: Continued advocacy

correspondence.recipient_title: President, Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians
correspondence.subject: Land purchase program and revolving loan fund
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledged Whiteman letter; indicated contact with Secretary Seaton; assured efforts to make funds available
correspondence.outcome: Sent telegram to Affiliated Tribes executive council meeting

correspondence.recipient_title: President, Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians
correspondence.subject: Executive Council meeting of Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians
correspondence.action_requested: Telegram wishing success at executive council meeting; assuring efforts on $15M loan fund
correspondence.outcome: Telegram sent to Spokane Hotel

correspondence.sender_title: President, Affiliated Tribes of Northwest Indians
correspondence.subject: Need for $50,000,000 appropriation bill for Indian land purchase programs
correspondence.action_requested: Requested Mansfield contact Secretary Seaton about administration support for $50M appropriation bill for Indian land purchase; also requested telegram to executive council meeting
correspondence.outcome: Mansfield responded February 10, 1960 and sent telegram

correspondence.subject: Rocky Boy revolving credit loan
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledged letter; assured continued efforts with Murray and Metcalf to make additional funds available
correspondence.outcome: Continued advocacy with Senate Appropriations Committee
